page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g7se.2xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 8 dedicated x86_64 vCPUs based on the Intel Xeon 8369B processor running at 2.9 GHz, paired with 32.0 GB of system memory. This configuration provides a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, allocating 4.0 GB of memory per core. The instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, relying entirely on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 3 Gbps.

The underlying Intel Xeon processor supports advanced instruction sets, including AVX, AVX512F, and AVX512_VNNI, which accelerate vector calculations and neural network instructions. Cryptographic operations are supported by AES, VAES, and SHA_NI instruction sets. Benchmark data indicates average performance across standard compute workloads, with a stress-ng single-core score of 1858.18 and a multi-core score of 7750.15. Memory bandwidth tests show 422.95 GB/sec for cached small-block reads, dropping to 58.09 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads and 38.1 GB/sec for large-block writes.

In application-specific benchmarks, the instance demonstrates moderate capabilities. It achieves a static web serving throughput of 11.25 GB/sec and processes 7.88 million Redis SET operations per second. Software compilation with Clang scores 8110.0, while text processing scores 2084.0 on Geekbench. For machine learning workloads, the instance handles small LLM inference (135M model) efficiently, processing 773.24 prompt tokens per second and generating 183.31 tokens per second. However, performance drops significantly on larger models, with a 7B model yielding only 15.66 prompt tokens per second and 6.62 text generation tokens per second, highlighting the hardware limitations for larger AI workloads.

The ecs.g7se.2xlarge offers a balanced resource profile suitable for general-purpose workloads, including web serving, database operations, and moderate serialization tasks. Its dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent performance without resource sharing, though the lack of local storage requires careful consideration of network-attached storage performance for I/O-heavy applications.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g7se.2xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance configured with 8 dedicated x86_64 vCPUs and 32.0 GB of RAM, delivering a balanced 1:4 resource ratio. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8369B processor running at 2.9 GHz, it supports advanced vector and cryptographic instructions such as AVX-512 and AES. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, utilizing a 3 Gbps baseline network bandwidth instead. Benchmark results place this instance in the average performance tier, with a static web serving throughput of 11.25 GB/sec and 7.88 million Redis SET operations per second. While it handles small-scale language model inference efficiently, performance degrades on larger models. The ecs.g7se.2xlarge is best suited for general-purpose applications, software compilation, web serving, and moderate database workloads requiring dedicated CPU resources.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g7se.2xlarge is an 8-vCPU, 32 GB RAM general-purpose instance featuring an Intel Xeon processor, 3 Gbps network bandwidth, and average performance across web serving and database workloads.

tagline: A balanced x86_64 compute instance featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and 32 GB RAM for general-purpose and web serving workloads.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated x86_64 vCPUs powered by Intel Xeon 8369B at 2.9 GHz
32.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
Supports AVX-512 and cryptographic acceleration instructions
Baseline network bandwidth of 3 Gbps with no local storage
Well-suited for web serving, software compilation, and database operations
